warnings: feedbackPathA .afternoon/chapters/chapter2/slop-gate-notes-r1a.json was not present; applied the available pass-B findings from slop-gate-notes-r1b.json.

changes.before: The way she said it made it sound like an illness.
The farther south they rode, the worse the countryside looked.
That possibility hit late and hard.
Millhaven lay ahead under the dying light, still as a trap.
Jaina gripped the staff and watched the village come up at them.
changes.after: Jaina gripped the staff as the village came up at them.

changes.before: The part of her that had always trusted being right to carry the day kept reaching for that certainty and finding nothing solid under it.
Cyndia had fallen half a step behind in the way subordinates do when authority is obvious and nobody needs to name it.
changes.after: Cyndia had fallen half a step behind.

changes.before: While Jaina sat on the bench waiting for someone worth talking to, this woman had already been inside the map.
changes.after: While Jaina had waited in the cell, Sylvanas had already traced the route.

changes.before: The part of her that had always trusted being right to carry the day kept reaching for that certainty and finding nothing solid under it.
The way she said it made it sound like an illness.
